_comment: RELATIVE / SELECTIVITY ABFE BENCHMARK for the custom ABFE engine (research/modalities/nr4a3_abfe.py). The reviewer's highest-value validation ask: a documented ONE-ligand / TWO-related-protein system with a KNOWN experimental selectivity DIRECTION — does our protocol recover it? Unlike the engine's ABSOLUTE benchmarks (methane hydration; T4-lysozyme/benzene), this tests the observable we actually use for decisions: a receptor-to-receptor ΔΔG for one ligand, in which a per-engine additive offset CANCELS. All experimental numbers below are REAL published values with citations (NOT synthetic). Staged by selectivity_benchmark_prep.py via .github/workflows/stage-selectivity-benchmark-aws.yml.
benchmark_type: relative (selectivity ΔΔG), one ligand across two related proteins

ligand.name: SGC-CBP30
ligand.smiles_source: AChemBlock / Selleck Chemicals vendor records; verified consistent with PubChem CID 72201027 formula C28H33ClN4O3 and the InChI above (1 stereocentre, S).

proteins.name: CREBBP (CBP) bromodomain
proteins.holo_pdb_title: Crystal structure of the bromodomain of human CREBBP in complex with an isoxazolyl-benzimidazole ligand (SGC-CBP30)

proteins.name: BRD4 bromodomain 1 (BD1)
proteins.holo_pdb_title: Crystal structure of BRD4 first bromodomain in complex with SGC-CBP30 chemical probe
proteins.kd_method: ITC (Kd ≈ 40x weaker than CREBBP; 21 nM x 40 ≈ 850 nM)

experimental_selectivity.direction: CREBBP binds SGC-CBP30 MORE TIGHTLY than BRD4(1)
experimental_selectivity.ddg_definition: ΔΔG_bind = ΔG_bind(CREBBP) − ΔG_bind(BRD4-1) = RT·ln(Kd_CREBBP / Kd_BRD4-1); negative => CREBBP more favourable.
experimental_selectivity.ddg_derivation: RT·ln(40) at T=298.15 K (RT=0.5925 kcal/mol): 0.5925 × ln(40) = 0.5925 × 3.6889 = 2.19 kcal/mol; sign negative for CREBBP-minus-BRD4.
experimental_selectivity.magnitude_note: ~2.2 kcal/mol is comfortably above ABFE statistical noise (typical selectivity SE ~0.3-0.6 kcal/mol per receptor) yet not so large as to be trivial — a genuine direction test.
experimental_selectivity.pass_criterion: Engine recovers the direction if computed ΔG_bind(crebbp) < ΔG_bind(brd4bd1) (i.e. computed ΔΔG_bind negative). A quantitative pass additionally puts |ΔΔG_calc − (−2.2)| within ~1 kcal/mol.

why_this_system: Reviewer implicitly pointed at bromodomain ABFE selectivity (Aldeghi et al., JACS 2017, 139:946). SGC-CBP30/CREBBP-vs-BRD4(1) IS a bromodomain selectivity contrast and is the cleanest such case with a SINGLE-primary-source, unambiguous ~40-fold directional difference.
ONE ligand, TWO close structural homologs (both bromodomain reader folds sharing the conserved Kac pocket) — exactly the one-ligand/two-related-protein design requested.
BOTH proteins crystallise HOLO with the SAME ligand SGC-CBP30 (4NR7, 5BT4), so each pose is lifted from a real crystal complex — no docking, no analog substitution.

Bromodomain selectivity is dominated by binding-SITE interactions (rigid domains, no large conformational reservoir), so crystal-pose ABFE captures the real selectivity determinant — unlike e.g. imatinib Abl/Src, whose selectivity is dominated by a DFG conformational term that crystal-pose ABFE famously misses.
The shared ligand makes the pipeline's single shared solvent leg (ligand-in-water) cancel EXACTLY between the two receptors — ideal for a ΔΔG.

citations.sgc_cbp30_discovery_affinity_selectivity: Hay, D. A.; Fedorov, O.; Martin, S.; Singleton, D. C.; Tallant, C.; Wells, C.; Picaud, S.; Philpott, M.; Monteiro, O. P.; Rogers, C. M.; Conway, S. J.; Rooney, T. P. C.; Tumber, A.; Yapp, C.; Filippakopoulos, P.; Bunnage, M. E.; Müller, S.; Knapp, S.; Schofield, C. J.; Brennan, P. E. 'Discovery and Optimization of Small-Molecule Ligands for the CBP/p300 Bromodomains.' J. Am. Chem. Soc. 2014, 136 (26), 9308-9319. DOI: 10.1021/ja412434f. (Reports SGC-CBP30: CREBBP Kd = 21 nM by ITC, ~40-fold selectivity over BRD4(1); deposited CREBBP holo structure 4NR7.)
citations.brd4bd1_holo_5BT4: RCSB PDB entry 5BT4, 'Crystal structure of BRD4 first bromodomain in complex with SGC-CBP30 chemical probe' (deposited with the SGC-CBP30 selectivity characterisation; see also EP300 holo 5BT3).
citations.probe_characterisation_context: Hammitzsch, A. et al. 'CBP30, a selective CBP/p300 bromodomain inhibitor, suppresses human Th17 responses.' Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 2015, 112 (34), 10768-10773. DOI: 10.1073/pnas.1501956112.
citations.reviewer_reference_abfe_selectivity_meth: Aldeghi, M.; Heifetz, A.; Bodkin, M. J.; Knapp, S.; Biggin, P. C. 'Predictions of Ligand Selectivity from Absolute Binding Free Energy Calculations.' J. Am. Chem. Soc. 2017, 139 (2), 946-957. DOI: 10.1021/jacs.6b11467. (Establishes ABFE selectivity prediction across bromodomains vs ITC — the methodological precedent this benchmark follows.)

staging.pose_lift: Heavy-atom coordinates from the holo HETATM records are lifted onto an RDKit molecule whose bond orders come from the isomeric SMILES (AssignBondOrdersFromTemplate); Hs added; _Name = 'sgc_cbp30'. The ligand copy chosen is the one in the SAME chain emitted as the apo PDB, so the pose sits in that protein copy's pocket.

abfe_plan.legs: per-receptor complex leg (complex-crebbp, complex-brd4bd1) + ONE shared solvent leg (ligand-in-water, identical for both receptors, cancels in ΔΔG).
abfe_plan.validate_first: Per CLAUDE.md validate-first: mode=smoke, then ONLY_LEGS=solvent (one real leg), then the full 3 legs.
abfe_plan.replicates: ≥1 replicate at seed=0; add seed=1,2 (mode=run, same tag) for a replicate-SD error bar on the ΔΔG if the single-replicate direction is marginal.
abfe_plan.reduce: mode=reduce (crebbp then brd4bd1) → ΔG_bind per receptor; ΔΔG_bind = ΔG_bind(crebbp) − ΔG_bind(brd4bd1). PASS if negative (CREBBP tighter).
abfe_plan.do_not_dispatch: This file + the staging job PREPARE the benchmark only. The expensive ABFE is dispatched by trimcrae after review.
